tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
tool_calls.content:
which nft && cat /etc/resolv.conf && ip route
/usr/sbin/nft
default via 192.168.1.1 dev eth0 
192.168.1.0/24 dev eth0 proto kernel scope link src 192.168.1.3 
203.0.113.0/24 dev eth1 proto kernel scope link src 203.0.113.2 
0;root@UbuntuComputer: /usr/src/approot@UbuntuComputer:/usr/src/app#

tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
tool_calls.arguments: {"device_identifier":"UbuntuComputer","command":"nft add table inet dnsguard; nft 'add chain inet dnsguard output { type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept; }'; nft add rule inet dnsguard output ip daddr 203.0.113.1 udp dport 8853 drop; nft add rule inet dnsguard output ip daddr 203.0.113.1 tcp dport 8853 drop; nft list chain inet dnsguard output"}

tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
send_command_to_device
tool_calls.content:
ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2; wget -q -T 4 --triees=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; echo $?; dig @203.0.113.1 -p 8853 test.example..com +time=1 +tries=1
PING 192.168.1.2 (192.168.1.2) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=7.93 ms

--- 192.168.1.2 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 7.926/7.926/7.926/0.000 ms
4
;; communications error to 203.0.113.1#8853: timed out
